scenario: Rails work session - audit the query Relation contract before a rework

map_quality.rationale: Names the monorepo shape, pins Relation to activerecord/lib/active_record/relation.rb with line numbers, and enumerates the mixed-in modules and value objects — the agent can pick entry points with only small lookups. Doesn't explicitly call out that finders/scopes/associations RETURN a Relation that accumulates and executes lazily, which is the orientation hook.

specificity.rationale: Cites concrete paths and line numbers (relation.rb:5, :54, :60, :63, :66, :68-70, :72, :88) and names the relation/ subdir files (where_clause.rb, from_clause.rb, merger.rb, predicate_builder/) rather than vague 'query layer' phrasing.

justification.rationale: Shows the structure (single @values hash, MULTI/SINGLE/CLAUSE buckets, mixed-in modules) but doesn't really explain WHY — that a finder/scope returns a lazy Relation chained and only executed on enumeration, or why the interface is split into focused modules. It names more than it justifies.

uncertainty.rationale: No flagging of uncertainty anywhere — no note about parts of the wiring (e.g. how Delegation routes to the model class, or how SignedId::RelationMethods plugs in) being unconfirmed. Reads as fully confident.

relationship.rationale: States the connecting relations partially: that the listed modules are 'mixed in at relation.rb:68-70' and that state lives in @values — this is more than a file list. But it doesn't say a finder/scope/association RETURNS a Relation, that chaining SPAWNS a new one, or that Delegation ROUTES to the model class — the orientation-level relations.

steps.step: Map the Relation contract and what it is built on

map_quality.rationale: Maps the contract comprehensively: state accessors, lifecycle, materialization, every mixed-in query module with file:line, value-object backings, and runtime subclassing/prepending. Agent has what it needs for small, targeted follow-ups.

specificity.rationale: Concrete file:line throughout — `relation.rb:72-76`, `relation/spawn_methods.rb:8`, `relation/delegation.rb:139`, `query_methods.rb:1673`, plus value-object files — not 'the query modules'.

justification.rationale: Explains WHY in the closing paragraph: '@values hash' as the chainable state, spawn/merge assuming `clone`+`initialize_copy` duplicating @values, subclassing/prepending shaping the contract. Slightly thin on why each module is split this way.

uncertainty.rationale: Flags one real hazard ('many callers reach it by bare Relation / === / is_a? rather than FQCN'), but doesn't explicitly mark anything as unconfirmed (e.g. token_for line, Calculations file:line).

relationship.rationale: States the binding relation per piece: each module's Role column ties it to what part of the query interface it supplies; Delegation's method-missing to model and per-class delegate cache; spawn/merge's clone+initialize_copy contract over @values. Chain from module → contract slot is explicit.

steps.step: Trace how a query is built up and executed

map_quality.rationale: The answer hands the agent the full build-and-execute path in order with file:line for every layer — factory (core.rb:442, relation.rb:78), finder/scope (scoping/named.rb:22,154), spawn (spawn_methods.rb:9, relation.rb:98), merge (spawn_methods.rb:33,43, merger.rb:43,175, where_clause.rb:26), Arel build (query_methods.rb:1673,1828), and execute (relation.rb:1212→1436→1460, querying.rb:67). The agent can follow it without re-exploration.

specificity.rationale: Pieces are named exactly (`SpawnMethods#spawn`, `Relation::Merger#merge_clauses`, `QueryMethods#arel`/`build_arel`/`build_joins`, `exec_queries`/`exec_main_query`, `_query_by_sql`) with concrete file:line, not vague 'the query code'.

justification.rationale: Some WHY is implicit (clone+dup in initialize_copy implies immutability of @values, `to_sql` renders without executing) but the answer doesn't explicitly say why chaining must spawn a fresh relation or why execution is deferred until enumeration.

uncertainty.rationale: No explicit flag of lazy parts or unconfirmed spawn/merge semantics — the path is asserted with full confidence even where Merger and clause merging have branches it didn't open.

relationship.rationale: The path is written as an explicit connected chain with arrows: factory → finder/scope → spawn (clone/initialize_copy) → merge (Merger/HashMerger/WhereClause) → Arel build → load/exec_queries/exec_main_query → select_all → instantiate_records. Order and route are both stated, not just a file set.

steps.step: Audit every dependent of the Relation contract

map_quality.rationale: Enumerates the full scattered set with file:line across all the required areas: query-interface modules, subclasses, the unqualified call sites (connection_handling, sanitization, integration cache key, encryption, signed_id, join_dependency, scoping/named, scoping/default), and the two cross-gem dependents (Enumerable#pick, railties query_command). Hits every area the rubric calls out.

specificity.rationale: Nearly every dependent has a concrete file:line and a phrase on how it uses the relation ('`ActiveRecord::Relation === value`', '`return_value.is_a? ActiveRecord::Relation` → forces `.load`', '`when ActiveRecord::Relation` → `.offset/.limit/.to_sql`'). Far beyond vague labels.

justification.rationale: Groups by area (A-D) and for most items pairs the citation with WHY it's affected (forces load under role, inlines via `===`, computes cache key over `all`, prepends RelationQueries, renders via `.to_sql`). A few citations in section C are just file:line without the why.

uncertainty.rationale: Does not explicitly flag what a static pass can miss (short-name references, `===` tests, cross-gem mirrors) — it surfaces them but doesn't tell the agent to verify or call out which entries are least certain.

relationship.rationale: For nearly every dependent it states the connecting relation: subclasses via `<`, `prepend(RelationQueries)`, `is_a?`/`===` type tests, `Delegation.delegated_classes` dynamic subclasses, mixin via `signed_id.rb:70`, Enumerable mirror of Calculations#pick. The chain from Relation to each dependent is explicit, including the cross-gem ones.

steps.step: Trace how relation behavior is reused for associations

map_quality.rationale: Traces the reuse path end-to-end with file:line: CollectionProxy<Relation, AssociationRelation<Relation, Association#scope→target_scope→AssociationScope.scope, plus the preload variant. Agent could change relation behavior knowing the association inheritance and scope-build path.

specificity.rationale: Concrete file:line throughout (collection_proxy.rb:31, association_relation.rb:4/:43, associations/association.rb:107/:302/:314, association_scope.rb:6/21, scoping/named.rb:36, preloader/association.rb:184/294) with exact symbols.

justification.rationale: Notes 'a collection IS a relation' and that AssociationRelation wraps exec_queries to set inverse instances, hinting at why subclassing is used, but doesn't explicitly explain WHY collections reuse Relation (to get the same chainable query interface).

uncertainty.rationale: No explicit flags about delegation/subclassing parts unverified; everything is asserted flatly with no hedge on the preload branch or the merge semantics.

relationship.rationale: States the connecting relations explicitly: CollectionProxy<Relation, AssociationRelation<Relation (with overrides), Association#scope→target_scope merges klass.scope_for_association then association_scope→AssociationScope.scope — an actual chain, not a file list.

steps.step: Assess the blast radius of the contract change

map_quality.rationale: Strong coverage with file:line across all areas: subclasses, prepend, spawn/merge core, execution, type-test sites, named scopes, and cross-gem dependents (railties query_command, activesupport enumerable.pick). Ranked by risk with clear high/medium/low tiers and an explicit re-verification list.

specificity.rationale: Nearly every dependent is cited with concrete file:line (e.g. `encryption/extended_deterministic_queries.rb:28`, `sanitization.rb:214`, `query_command.rb:174`, `enumerable.rb:161`) and a stated reason like `is_a?`/`===`/`when ActiveRecord::Relation`.

justification.rationale: Each tier explains WHY: subclasses hit via `super`, prepend relies on internal names, spawn/merge assumes `clone`+`@values` dup, type-tests break if spawn changes class. Risk distinctions are grounded in mechanism, not just labels.

uncertainty.rationale: Flags cross-gem dependents as 'easy to miss, no FQCN-by-default' which acknowledges the static-pass blind spot, but doesn't explicitly call out other unverified areas (e.g. predicate_builder handlers are listed without confidence).

relationship.rationale: Each at-risk dependent is tied to the relationship that makes it risky — `super` chain through subclasses, prepend on Relation, `clone`+`@values` dup in spawn, `is_a?`/`===` type tests, `when ActiveRecord::Relation` in the railties case statement, `pick` parity with Calculations — turning the list into connected impact paths rather than labeled files.

map_quality.rationale: Comprehensive map: contract files with line numbers, every dependent area (subclasses, association build, scattered call sites, runtime prepend, cross-gem), and the full test suite list including railties query command test and activesupport enumerable_test. A teammate could land the change from this alone with only small lookups.

specificity.rationale: Concrete file:line throughout — `relation.rb:78/98/1212/1436/1460`, `sanitization.rb:214`, `connection_handling.rb:419`, `signed_id.rb:33/37`, `railties/.../query_command.rb:174`, `activesupport/.../enumerable.rb:161`, plus specific test files like `collection_cache_key_test.rb` and `scoping/named_scoping_test.rb`.

justification.rationale: Risk ranking ties files to *why* they matter (subclasses+delegate cache, encryption prepend, spawn/merge core, type-test sites), and grouping by area implies the role. But many test entries are listed without an explicit per-file reason for inclusion.

uncertainty.rationale: Closes with 'Add coverage for ... the `is_a?/===` contract if the spawned class changes' which flags an unsure area, and the JSON `step6_blast_radius.risk: high` implies caution. No explicit call-out of which cross-gem or unqualified dependents are least confirmed.

relationship.rationale: Each file is tied to the change via its connecting relation: `connection_role_shard_exec: connection_handling.rb:419 (is_a? ActiveRecord::Relation -> .load)`, `sql_bind_variable: sanitization.rb:214 (=== value)`, `encrypted_query_rewrite: ... prepend, :44 is_a?`, plus the explicit build/exec chain. Cross-gem ties (`activesupport_core_ext_pick`, `railties_query_renderer`) state the role, though tests are mostly grouped without per-test path mapping.
